request.source_summary: Create a small Codex agent team that researches 12 to 15 current agent frameworks, prioritizes open-source options, includes a small number of representative official or commercial platforms, and delivers a comparison matrix, ranked selection conclusion, and phased implementation roadmap.

workflow_detection.summary: No workflow-specific profile was required; this team uses a generic research-and-synthesis lifecycle.

assumptions: Used agent-framework-landscape-brief.md as the primary request source.
The generated first pass was refined manually because keyword heuristics collapsed the research workflow into generic implementation tasks.
Generated .toml files remain reviewable drafts and are not installed automatically.
Open-source frameworks remain the priority, while official or commercial platforms are included only as representative context.

task_graph.title: Freeze Scope And Rubric
task_graph.description: Lock the framework sample pool, evaluation dimensions, evidence standard, and merge rules before parallel research starts.

task_graph.title: Research Open-Source Frameworks
task_graph.description: Research the primary open-source agent frameworks against the frozen rubric and capture evidence-backed framework cards.

task_graph.title: Research Representative Official Or Commercial Platforms
task_graph.description: Research a small number of representative official or commercial agent platforms for context using the same rubric and evidence standard.

task_graph.title: Normalize Comparison Matrix
task_graph.description: Merge the two research tracks into one normalized matrix with aligned scoring language, comparable evidence links, and explicit gaps.

task_graph.title: Recommendation And Roadmap
task_graph.description: Turn the normalized matrix into recommended tiers, scenario-specific picks, and a phased proof-of-concept to adoption roadmap.

task_graph.title: Final Package Review
task_graph.description: Check that the matrix, recommendation, and roadmap are evidence-backed, mutually consistent, and scoped to the requested deliverables.

parallel_groups.goal: Run evidence gathering in parallel after the rubric is frozen.
parallel_groups.why_parallelizable: Both tasks are read-heavy, use the same rubric, and write to separate evidence bundles that merge only in the matrix stage.
parallel_groups.merge_point: Lead confirms both evidence bundles are complete enough for analyst normalization before synthesis begins.

agents.name: lead
agents.purpose: Own scope freeze, rubric integrity, merge points, and final delivery review.
agents.developer_instructions: Keep the team aligned on the frozen rubric, reject unsupported conclusions, and ensure every final recommendation is traceable back to the matrix.

agents.name: explorer-open-source
agents.purpose: Research the open-source framework landscape with evidence-backed framework cards.
agents.developer_instructions: Stay read-only, prioritize official docs and repositories, and report comparable evidence for each framework against the frozen rubric.

agents.name: explorer-commercial
agents.purpose: Research representative official or commercial agent platforms for context and contrast.
agents.developer_instructions: Stay read-only, keep coverage intentionally selective, and focus on representative platforms that sharpen the final comparison rather than expanding the sample count without bound.

agents.name: analyst
agents.purpose: Normalize both research tracks into one comparison matrix with aligned scoring and explicit evidence gaps.
agents.developer_instructions: Do not reopen research scope. Normalize terminology, flag missing evidence, and keep the matrix comparable across all frameworks and platforms.

agents.name: strategist
agents.purpose: Convert the matrix into ranked recommendations, scenario-specific picks, and a phased implementation roadmap.
agents.developer_instructions: Base every recommendation on matrix evidence, separate general-purpose picks from scenario-specific picks, and keep the roadmap constrained to realistic proof-of-concept and adoption phases.

prompts.prompt: Use the lead agent to freeze the sample pool, evaluation rubric, merge rules, and final review boundary for the agent-framework research package.
Use the explorer-open-source agent to research the open-source agent frameworks with official-source evidence and write framework cards against the frozen rubric.
Use the explorer-commercial agent to research a small number of representative official or commercial platforms and write comparable evidence cards without expanding scope unnecessarily.
Use the analyst agent to merge both research tracks into one normalized comparison matrix, align terminology, and flag evidence gaps.
Use the strategist agent to turn the normalized matrix into ranked recommendations, scenario-specific picks, and a phased implementation roadmap.

lead.toml.content:
name = "lead"
description = "Own scope freeze, rubric integrity, merge points, and final delivery review."
model = "gpt-5.4"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
tool_output_token_limit = 40000
model_reasoning_summary = "detailed"
model_verbosity = "high"
model_supports_reasoning_summaries = true
service_tier = "fast"
nickname_candidates = ["Atlas", "Beacon", "Helm"]
developer_instructions = """
Keep the team aligned on the frozen rubric, reject unsupported conclusions, and ensure every final recommendation is traceable back to the matrix.
"""

execution_plan.title: Freeze Scope And Rubric
execution_plan.handoff: Lead publishes the rubric, sample pool, and evidence standard before any parallel research starts.

execution_plan.title: Research Open-Source Frameworks
execution_plan.handoff: Explorer-open-source hands off evidence-backed framework cards to the analyst after lead confirms scope coverage.

execution_plan.title: Research Representative Official Or Commercial Platforms
execution_plan.handoff: Explorer-commercial hands off comparable platform cards to the analyst after lead confirms the representative set is sufficient.

execution_plan.title: Normalize Comparison Matrix
execution_plan.handoff: Analyst publishes the normalized matrix and gap log before any recommendations are drafted.

execution_plan.title: Recommendation And Roadmap
execution_plan.handoff: Strategist drafts the recommendation package and roadmap using only matrix-backed claims.

execution_plan.title: Final Package Review
execution_plan.handoff: Lead checks cross-document consistency, traceability, and scope before delivery.

risks: If the lead does not freeze the rubric up front, the two research tracks will drift and the matrix will become non-comparable.
Allowing either explorer to expand the framework list opportunistically will break the requested 12 to 15 framework budget and slow the synthesis stages.
If the analyst silently normalizes missing evidence into ratings, the selection conclusion will look precise without being trustworthy.
Recommendation and roadmap work must stay serial after matrix normalization; starting them early would create unsupported conclusions.
open_questions: Which 2 to 4 official or commercial platforms should be treated as representative context during stage 1 scope freeze?
Should the final package include weighted scoring, or should the matrix remain qualitative with scenario-based recommendations?
Do you want these TOML drafts to stay local artifacts, or should they later be promoted into persistent Codex agent configs after the team proves useful?
